summary:
Benign intracranial hypertension (IIH) is characterized by headaches, papilledema, normal-sized ventricles, and responsiveness to weight loss or acetazolamide. Proptosis is not a feature of IIH and is instead associated with orbital pathologies such as Graves' ophthalmopathy or tumor-related conditions. Thus, while all options relate to treatment aspects, corticosteroids are not first-line; they have limited efficacy compared to lifestyle modifications and medications like acetazolamide.  

The false statement is therefore D because corticosteroids are not considered first-line therapy—they lack strong support in treating IIH versus established treatments including dietary changes and carbonic anhydrase inhibitors. Correctly identifying non-standard therapies helps avoid inappropriate use based solely on familiarity without evidence backing.

The false statement is therefore D because corticosteroids are not considered first-line therapy—they lack strong support in treating IIH versus established treatments including dietary changes and carbonic anhydrase inhibitors.
